Katalon Marketing Mix Model — V2 Bayesian

Hill-adstock model with NUTS sampling  ·  March 25, 2026

Bayesian hill-adstock (NumPyro NUTS) 200 warmup + 500 samples × 1 chain 3 outcomes V1 OLS comparison included

Model Comparison — V1 OLS vs V2 Bayesian

Both models fit the same data and lag settings. R² and MAPE measure in-sample fit quality. V2 Bayesian adds uncertainty estimates (credible intervals) on all parameters.

Hill Saturation Curves

Learned diminishing-returns curves. K = half-saturation point (scaled spend at 50% of max response). α = shape: >1 = S-curve, <1 = concave.

What Bayesian Adds Over OLS

V1 OLS (baseline)

  • Fixed adstock decay rates (hand-tuned per channel)
  • Linear saturation — assumes ROI constant across spend levels
  • Point estimates only — no uncertainty on attribution
  • Fast: runs in under 1 second
  • Interpretable: OLS coefficients map directly to attribution

V2 Bayesian (this report)

  • Adstock decay learned from data (posterior distribution)
  • Hill saturation captures diminishing returns explicitly
  • Credible intervals on every ROI and attribution estimate
  • Informative priors prevent overfitting on small sample (n≈46)
  • Slower: ~8s per outcome in quick mode, ~60s in full mode
When to trust V2 more: When V2 R² ≥ V1 R² and the credible intervals are narrow. Wide 80% CIs (e.g. ROI 0.1x–15x) mean the data is insufficient to pin down that channel's effect — the posterior is prior-dominated.
ROI uncertainty caveat: With ~40–46 observations and 9 channels, Bayesian credible intervals are wide. Use ROI rankings (relative ordering) rather than absolute ROI values for budget decisions.
